name: Bangkok
localTips: Tap a contactless bank card or grab a Rabbit Card for the BTS Skytrain — traffic makes the Skytrain/MRT combo the fastest way across the city by far.
Dress modestly for temples — covered shoulders and knees are required at the Grand Palace and Wat Pho; sarongs are sold or rented at the gate if you forget.
Always insist on the meter in a taxi or just use Grab — tuk-tuks love to quote flat 'tourist' fares and detour to gem shops that pay them commission.
Street food is safest where the queue is long and the cart parks in the same spot every day — that's a business built on repeat local customers, not tourists.
The BTS and MRT stop around midnight; after that it's taxis, Grab, or the 24-hour noodle stalls of Chinatown until the trains restart.

activities.title: Wat Arun view from Tha Tien pier
activities.description: The porcelain-encrusted spires of the Temple of Dawn glow across the river from the free public pier at Tha Tien — best at sunset when the tiles catch the light.

activities.title: Chatuchak Weekend Market
activities.description: Fifteen thousand stalls across 27 sections — vintage denim, ceramics, snakes-in-a-bag pets and a food alley locals actually eat at, all under one sprawling roof network.

activities.title: Lumpini Park
activities.description: Bangkok's central green lung — paddleboats on the lake, outdoor gyms full of locals at dawn, and monitor lizards the size of dogs cruising the paths.

activities.title: Yaowarat Chinatown street food & neon
activities.description: Bangkok's Chinatown strip explodes into neon-lit wok smoke after dark — grilled satay, bird's nest soup stalls and gold shops that never seem to close.

activities.title: Khao San Road backpacker strip
activities.description: The world's backpacker crossroads since the 1980s — fried insects, bucket cocktails, tattoo stalls and street bands playing until the small hours.

activities.title: Erawan Shrine
activities.description: A gilded four-faced Brahma shrine wedged between shopping malls — hired Thai classical dancers perform continuously as offerings for granted wishes.

activities.title: Asiatique The Riverfront
activities.description: A converted 19th-century trading pier turned open-air night market — a giant Ferris wheel, warehouse boutiques and riverside restaurants, free to walk through.

activities.title: Benjakitti Forest Park
activities.description: A former tobacco factory reclaimed into elevated boardwalks through wetland forest, skyline views included, right behind the QSNCC convention centre.

activities.title: Bang Krachao cycling loop
activities.description: A five-minute cross-river ferry from the skyscrapers drops you in Bangkok's 'green lung' — an island of stilted houses, mangroves and empty cycling lanes.

activities.title: Chao Phraya Express Boat ride
activities.description: The commuter ferry that locals use daily — a few baht buys a breezy, traffic-free ride past the Grand Palace, Wat Arun and riverside temples.

activities.title: Ratchadamnoen Avenue & Democracy Monument
activities.description: Bangkok's Champs-Élysées — a broad boulevard of 1930s monumental architecture leading from the Democracy Monument toward the old royal quarter.

activities.title: Or Tor Kor Market
activities.description: A covered, air-cooled market rated among the world's best by CNN — immaculate fruit stalls, ready-to-eat curries and durian vendors who'll pick the ripe one for you.

activities.title: Siam Square street style walk
activities.description: Bangkok's teen fashion capital — narrow sois of independent boutiques and photo studios wedged between the mega-malls of Siam Paragon and CentralWorld.

activities.title: Pak Khlong Talat flower market
activities.description: A 24-hour riverside flower market where orchid garlands and marigold mountains are sorted through the night — most atmospheric between midnight and dawn.

activities.title: ICONSIAM & SookSiam floating market
activities.description: A riverside mega-mall with a full-scale floating-market replica indoors — browse SookSiam's regional food stalls and boat vendors without leaving air conditioning.

activities.title: Jim Thompson House Museum
activities.description: The teak stilt-house compound of the American silk entrepreneur who vanished in Malaysia's jungle in 1967, filled with his Southeast Asian art collection.
activities.insiderNote: The garden café at the back does an excellent khao soi and is far less crowded than the museum shop side — a good lunch stop most tourists skip.

activities.title: Soi Nana speakeasy bars
activities.description: Not the red-light Soi Nana — this Chinatown backstreet hides multi-award-winning cocktail bars like Tep Bar and Teens of Thailand behind unmarked shophouse doors.
activities.insiderNote: Tep Bar serves Thai spirits like sato rice wine with live traditional music some nights — arrive before 8pm to get a seat without a wait.

activities.title: On Lok Yun old-school coffee shop
activities.description: A 1933 Hainanese coffee shop near the old Chinese-Portuguese quarter — thick toast with kaya, soft-boiled eggs and coffee served the way Bangkok has drunk it for a century.
activities.insiderNote: Order the eggs 'half-boiled' the local way and crack them into a cup with soy sauce and white pepper rather than eating them from the shell.

activities.title: Bang Nam Pheung weekend floating market
activities.description: A tiny, mostly-local floating market in Bang Krachao's mangrove loop — boat noodles cooked from the water and none of the coach-tour crowds of Damnoen Saduak.
activities.insiderNote: Combine it with the Bang Krachao bike loop — the market is one stop on the island, best reached by bicycle before 10am.

activities.title: Talad Rot Fai Ratchada night market
activities.description: A neon-soaked train-yard-turned-market under a covered vendor grid — vintage VW campers as bars, retro toys and a beer garden locals actually go to on weeknights.
activities.insiderNote: Skip the main aisle food and head to the shipping-container bar zone at the back for cheaper drinks and live music.

activities.title: Soi Texas Chinatown seafood grills
activities.description: A covered alley of open-fire seafood restaurants competing wok-to-wok — point at the crab, prawns or scallops in the tanks and watch them grilled to order.
activities.insiderNote: Lek & Rut and T&K Seafood are the famous names, but the smaller stalls between them charge less for nearly identical food.

activities.title: Muay Thai gym morning training
activities.description: Neighbourhood gyms like Sor Vorapin open their morning sessions to visitors — watch pad work and clinch drills up close, or join a beginner class yourself.
activities.insiderNote: Call ahead — most gyms let you watch for free if you're not training, but a small donation to the gym is good form.

activities.title: Warehouse 30 creative district
activities.description: Restored WWII-era river warehouses turned into design studios, indie cinema and a specialty coffee roaster — Bangkok's creative-industry crowd, not the tour buses.
activities.insiderNote: Kinn Kafe inside does an excellent Thai iced tea affogato and the exposed timber-truss ceiling makes it a great rainy-afternoon hideout.

activities.title: Grand Palace & Wat Pho guided tour
activities.description: The dazzling former royal residence and the Emerald Buddha temple, paired with the 46-metre reclining Buddha at Wat Pho next door — a guide navigates the crowds and dress code.
activities.bookingQuery: Grand Palace Wat Pho guided tour Bangkok

activities.title: Chao Phraya dinner cruise
activities.description: A covered river cruise past the illuminated Grand Palace and Wat Arun, with a Thai buffet and live band on board — a relaxed way to see the riverside skyline.

activities.title: Damnoen Saduak floating market day trip
activities.description: The classic postcard floating market an hour outside the city — boatwomen in straw hats paddling produce and noodle soup down narrow klong canals.
activities.bookingQuery: Damnoen Saduak floating market day tour from Bangkok

activities.title: Ayutthaya ancient city day trip
activities.description: The ruined 14th-century capital of Siam — headless Buddha statues, brick chedis swallowed by tree roots at Wat Mahathat, and a river that once hosted a thousand ships.

activities.title: Muay Thai fight night at Rajadamnern Stadium
activities.description: The world's oldest Muay Thai stadium, opened in 1945 — ringside seats put you close enough to hear the pad strikes over the live musical accompaniment.
activities.bookingQuery: Muay Thai fight night ticket Rajadamnern Stadium Bangkok

activities.title: Thai cooking class with market visit
activities.description: Shop for ingredients at a wet market with your instructor, then cook pad thai, green curry and tom yum from scratch in a hands-on kitchen class.
activities.bookingQuery: Thai cooking class with market visit Bangkok

activities.title: Tuk-tuk night food tour
activities.description: A driver-guide zips you between five or six local-only eating spots by tuk-tuk — from a hidden noodle shop to a dessert stall, skipping the tourist menus entirely.

activities.title: Rooftop sky bar reservation
activities.description: Open-air rooftop bars like Sky Bar at Lebua or Vertigo at Banyan Tree perch 60-plus floors up — reserve ahead for a sunset table over the glittering skyline.

activities.title: Maeklong Railway Market day trip
activities.description: Vendors fold back their awnings within seconds as a train rumbles through the middle of a working market — combine with Damnoen Saduak for a full-day tour.
activities.bookingQuery: Maeklong Railway Market day tour from Bangkok

activities.title: Thonburi canal longtail boat tour
activities.description: A roaring longtail boat cuts through Thonburi's klong network past stilted wooden houses and a floating market stop — the old Bangkok that predates the skyscrapers.
activities.bookingQuery: Thonburi canal longtail boat tour Bangkok
